summary: Russian forces reported the liberation of Yurkovka in the DPR and continued advances in Konstantinovka, seizing dozens of buildings and strongpoints. Ukrainian sources detailed heavy Russian drone activity across multiple oblasts and a strike on a logistics facility in Zaporizhzhia that injured eight. Both sides reported significant casualties and equipment losses in the contested areas.
key_points: Russian troops liberated Yurkovka in the DPR, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31025).
Russian forces occupied 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and seized 37 strongholds in Krasny Liman, reporting over 185 Ukrainian casualties there (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian drones were observed near Kropyvnytskyi, Kharkiv, and across several other regions, indicating widespread UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/65686, @kpszsu/65659).
A Russian drone strike on a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia injured eight people, including a police officer (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Russian forces claimed control of the sole road from Druzkovka to Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/381592).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 76 drones near Moscow and additional UAVs in Tula, Sochi, Crimea, and Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian troops reported taking five Ukrainian strongpoints in Krasny Liman and clearing 47 buildings (Source: @tass_agency/381586).
Multiple Ukrainian regions reported ongoing launches of strike UAVs, including from Dnipro, Sumy, and Cherkasy areas (Source: @eRadarrua/97370, @kpszsu/65653).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces announced the liberation of Yurkovka in the DPR and significant gains in Konstantinovka, seizing hundreds of buildings and dozens of strongpoints. Ukrainian sources reported extensive UAV activity across several oblasts and a drone strike on a logistics facility in Zaporizhzhia that injured eight. Both sides cited notable casualties and equipment losses, indicating heightened combat intensity.

points: Ukrainian drone operators reported a strike on a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia, causing eight injuries, including a police officer, and damaging vehicles and the structure (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 76 drones near Moscow and additional UAVs over Tula, Sochi, Crimea, and Sevastopol, reflecting intensified aerial threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing UAV launches from Dnipro, Sumy, and Cherkasy regions, indicating sustained offensive air operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97370, @kpszsu/65653).
Ukrainian reports indicated that Russian troops seized 37 strongholds in Krasny Liman and over 185 Ukrainian personnel were killed there (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian sources claimed that Russian forces occupied 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and eliminated surrounded Ukrainian units in its southwestern sector (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced the liberation of Yurkovka in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31025).
Russian troops reported taking five Ukrainian strongpoints in Krasny Liman and clearing 47 buildings (Source: @tass_agency/381586).
Russian forces claimed control of the only road linking Druzkovka to Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/381592).

Russian statements indicated that assault groups were eliminating surrounded Ukrainian units in the southwestern part of Konstantinovka and advancing in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Russian defense officials reported that UAV attacks were conducted in the Tula region, Sochi, Crimea, and Sevastopol, with some drones intercepted (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Kropyvnytskyi moving northwest, indicating activity in the central-eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65686).
Strike UAVs were reported heading toward Pavlohrad from eastern Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/65653).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV movements past Sumy toward Stepanivka and Nedryhayliv from the southeast (Source: @kpszsu/65669).
Russian troops reported occupying 627 buildings in Konstantinovka, a key eastern town (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian reports indicated Russian seizure of 37 strongholds in Krasny Liman, an eastern settlement (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
UAVs were reported flying past Kulykivka in Chernihiv region, heading toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65683).
UAV activity noted near Korop in Chernihiv, moving southwest (Source: @kpszsu/65679).
UAV groups observed near the Sumy‑Chernihiv border moving southwest toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65656).
UAVs were seen over the Kyiv water reservoir and circling the Oster/Desna area (Source: @eRadarrua/97409).
Russian forces claimed control of a strategic road from Druzkovka to Konstantinovka, affecting northern supply lines (Source: @tass_agency/381592).
UAVs were reported in Kharkiv region near Bohodukhiv heading southeast toward Izium and Zolotopil (Source: @kpszsu/65659).
UAV activity noted in Kherson region moving northwest toward Mykolaiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65659).
Ukrainian strike UAVs were observed near Kryvyi Rih heading west toward Kirovohrad (Source: @kpszsu/65683).
Russian troops reported seizing five Ukrainian strongpoints in Krasny Liman, a southern settlement (Source: @tass_agency/381586).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia, causing eight injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 76 drones near Moscow and additional UAVs over Tula, Sochi, Crimea, and Sevastopol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Russian forces reported UAV attacks in the Tula region, with some drones intercepted (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed conducting strikes across multiple oblasts, including Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, and Cherkasy (Source: @eRadarrua/97370, @kpszsu/65653).
Russian troops used UAVs to monitor and support ground advances in Konstantinovka and Krasny Liman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
A Russian UAV attack on a logistics facility in Zaporizhzhia resulted in eight injuries and damage to vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).

key_developments: Russian troops liberated Yurkovka in the DPR, expanding their control in the region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31025).
Russian forces occupied 627 buildings in Konstantinovka and seized 37 strongholds in Krasny Liman, indicating deep territorial gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31026).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 76 drones near Moscow and additional UAVs over Tula, Sochi, Crimea, and Sevastopol, showing heightened aerial engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31042).
A Russian drone strike on a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia injured eight, including a police officer, and caused structural damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported across multiple oblasts, including Kropyvnytskyi, Kharkiv, and Kherson, indicating sustained offensive operations (Source: @kpszsu/65686, @kpszsu/65659).
Russian forces claimed control of the sole road from Druzkovka to Konstantinovka, affecting Ukrainian logistics (Source: @tass_agency/381592).
Russian troops reported taking five Ukrainian strongpoints in Krasny Liman and clearing 47 buildings, consolidating their hold (Source: @tass_agency/381586).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating gains in the DPR and eastern Ukraine, seizing key towns and infrastructure while maintaining pressure on supply routes such as the Druzkovka‑Konstantinovka road. Ukrainian UAV operations remain active across multiple fronts, providing reconnaissance and strike capabilities but also exposing assets to Russian air defenses. The increased frequency of drone attacks on logistical hubs, exemplified by the Zaporizhzhia strike, raises the risk of further civilian casualties and infrastructure disruption.
sections.regional_response: Neighboring states are monitoring the escalation, with no direct military involvement reported in the provided data. Russian claims of territorial advances may influence diplomatic negotiations, while Ukraine's reliance on UAVs underscores the need for continued external support to sustain air defense and strike capabilities. The ongoing combat and civilian impact could affect regional stability and humanitarian conditions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open-source UAV tracking indicates a pattern of coordinated strikes targeting logistics and command nodes, suggesting a strategic focus on disrupting Ukrainian supply chains. Russian reports of building seizures and road control point to a methodical approach to consolidating territorial gains. Continued monitoring of drone activity and casualty reports is essential for assessing operational tempo and forecasting future engagements.
